title: Exercise Specialist (Rehab)
description:
Our client is expanding their team and they are looking for Exercise Specialists to join them. As an Exercise Specialist, you will empower people to live better by moving better through exercise.

The successful candidate will report to the Functional Rehab department lead.

What the role offers:

  • Training to sharpen your exercise prescription skills;
  • Learn from professionals within the team; and
  • Application of your skills so clients are able to maximise their musculoskeletal rehab potential and beyond.

Primary job responsibilities:

  • Support the clinical team by executing the plans of the case lead;
  • Guide clients with appropriate exercises and instruction; and
  • Effectively communicate with members in the team so that the clients receive a clear plan forward and a high standard of care.

Expectations of the role:

  • Pass the in-house training programme within two tries
  • Work with case leads to get referrals
  • Work in a multi-disciplinary team;
  • Plan and execute exercises and sessions in consult with case leads;
  • Ensure clients complete their rehab journey;
  • Provide the team with timely updates on client’s progress.

Requirements:

  • Passion in working with clients in a musculoskeletal (MSK) setting and helping them; maximise their rehab potential;
  • Basic knowledge of anatomy and exercise physiology;
  • Good learning attitude; strong growth mindset;
  • Possesses the desire and ability to learn (e.g. inquisitive, open to feedback);
  • Keen to apply their learning;
  • Good interpersonal skills;
  • Demonstrates ability to speak and communicate well; and
  • Team player mindset.

The role is open to:

  • Personal Trainers/group exercise instructors looking for a stable career in a rehab role.
  • Sports science/physiology uni grads from NTU, PSB, Deakin, Edith Cowen, UWA; Fresh graduates are welcome to apply.
  • Hospital Therapy Assistants (TAs) doing musculoskeletal (MSK) rehab
  • Practitioners from private MSK/chiro clinics looking to do more exercise-related work rather than using machine modalities

Work hours:

5.5-day work week (alternate Saturdays) :

  • Mondays to Fridays, 9am-6pm (subject to client's schedule) AND
  • Alternate Saturdays 9am-1pm

Work location: The location assigned will be based on company's needs and candidate's suitability. The locations may include Raffles Place, HarbourFront, or Bukit Timah.

Highlights

  • In-house training provided; confirmation of role will be dependent of completing and passing the training
  • Bonus
  • Medical/Dental benefits
  • Annual Leave

Apply now if you have the relevant training/experience and passionate about helping people live better by moving better.

For clarifications, please contact Karyn Choo (EA Personnel Reg: R24120779) karyn@jobsjolt.com
